reported_result: Expected field belonging: F(1, 322) = 5.17, p = .02, eta_p2 = .02
raw_variable_names: IV_profgender; IV_safetycues; fieldexp_1-fieldexp_9; fieldexp_4 reverse-coded; exact nearest-observed 3-SD winsorization
model_formula: fieldbelong_exact_winsorized ~ IV_profgender * IV_safetycues; Type-III SPSS UNIANOVA logic with sum contrasts
contrast_direction: identity-safety-cue syllabus versus control syllabus on expected social-psychology field belonging
extraction_note: The article result is reproduced by reconstructing the nine-item composite, reverse-scoring fieldexp_4 as 6-fieldexp_4, and replacing the two values beyond three SD with the nearest observed non-outlier values (participant 320: 2 to 2.333333333333; participant 128: 5 to 4.888888888889). Result: F(1, 322) = 5.169119765850, p = 0.0236499529367, eta_p2 = 0.015799534411. Control n = 163, safety n = 163; control M = 3.472392638037, safety M = 3.593047034765. The separate SPSS screenshot used the un-winsorized composite and therefore shows F = 5.320573648228 rather than the article's F = 5.17.

reported_result: Inclusive classroom impression: F(1, 322) = 42.95, p < .001, eta_p2 = .12
raw_variable_names: IV_profgender; IV_safetycues; include_1; author-saved processed inclusive-classroom score
model_formula: include_1 ~ IV_profgender * IV_safetycues; Type-III SPSS UNIANOVA logic with sum contrasts
contrast_direction: identity-safety-cue syllabus versus control syllabus on perception that the professor is trying to create an inclusive classroom environment
extraction_note: The author-saved include_1 variable reproduces the article and SPSS output: F(1, 322) = 42.951850798083, p = 2.22660053111e-10, eta_p2 = 0.117691828947. Control n = 163, safety n = 163; control M = 77.656441717791, safety M = 90.447852760736. The distributed SAV contains the processed include_1 values, so the original pre-winsorization observation cannot be recovered from this file alone.
